name: Allen, Willard Increase Jr.
description: This nutmeg is not a regular at college, that is to say, he is not in with the bunch but he is here at all athletic games all right. His voice is second only to T Hall bell when it rings at seven A.M. The Venerable Order of Dogs had no effect on that voice when they washed it with a formula known only to members of the order. Along with the voice goes an unbeatable appetite. He could clean a table d'hote lunch absolutely free from all victuals.

name: Atkins, Harry Clifton
description: Charlie left the dear old farm and came to us determined to take the electrical engineering course, but at the end of the first semester he was lured into the chem. course by the "King," and, if any conclusions can be drawn from his remarks, he will graduate in the agricultural division. Civy once showed his knowledge of chemistry by demonstrating that NaNO₃ was an economical substitute for NaCl in freezing ice cream.

name: Austin, Charles Oakley
motto: "In dear old Georgia, my southern home"
description: From the deepest wilds of Georgia, across the Mason and Dixon line, via Laconia and Dover, comes the cotton picker and mule driver. Dutch has started back to his old haunts several times, but always fails to make connections at Dover. One of the prominent figures on the Weirs Boulevard last summer was Charles Oakley and his 'lectric. Being ambidextrous Dutch can take notes with the best of them and speed is what he likes.

name: Bachelder, Henry Putney
description: Although H. P. hasn't shown us that he intends to bring the governorship of the state back to the Bachelder family, he did perk up considerably at the time of the presidential election. At that time his redoubtable and versatile wife, the famous Heinie, got some real speed out of him, but he has long since drifted back to his old-time habits. Batch's home ties must be very strong ones, for we never yet have known of his going to Dover when it wasn't absolutely necessary. He has never taken a library course or been to an informal, but he's "a man for a' that."

name: Badger, Phillips Brooks
description: The Portsmouth farmer has had a keen eye for knowledge of the soil. The mail is stuffed with bulletins from distant lands and catalogues from all sorts of "Aggie" concerns. Sam has to carry a heavy supply of postal cards to satisfy this lad's desires. Pony's only excuse is that he hasn't the time. Otherwise he might have been one of New Hampshire's stars. If you want to learn a little about etiquette, just watch the boy get his beans in with his mud hooks, and also suck coffee through a doughnut.

name: Baker, John Arthur
motto: "He had hard words ready to show why, And tell what deeds he did it by"
description: There was nothing in the E. E. course that Baker didn't already know, so he elected Chem as a side-issue to his thesis work on wireless telegraphy. John was never caught swearing during his Freshman year, but now there is a different story to tell. Aside from this human failing, you might easily mistake him for a profound theological student.

name: Bartlett, Richard Cilley
motto: I A.M. to bed, 8 A.M. to rise, Makes Dick elongated instead of wise
description: As a Freshman Dick was somewhat of a kid and didn't realize what he came to college for. However, he managed to "Weigel" through his Sophomore year with flying colors and now stands 6 feet 3 with the profs. His far-fetched humor is only appreciated by his (B) lackie room-mate. That flute played a mean trick on him by making him drill all winter with the band! Women and "Red Ink" are all the same to Dick.

name: Batchelder, Philip
motto: Crust enough to start a bakery
description: One of the lesser renowned members of the "We Scientists" Club is Phil. Although a pronounced ladies' man at present and a visitor both in Exeter and Haverhill, he still vows that he will always remain a "Batchelder." From the looks of Phil's schedule one would think that he was specializing in Home Ec. One good point in his favor is the fact that he has changed his place of residence. (He formerly lived in Laconia.)

name: Bell, Ernest Lorne Jr.
motto: An inch to the left-ah, and an inch to the right-ah, and I wouldn't be wandering here tonight-ah
description: Little Ernie made a stab at the Engineering Course, but along about the first of February he decided that the Arts division was nearer his style. Doc has gained the good-will of all his profs—but why shouldn't he, his uncle was a bull-fighter.

name: Bennett, Charles Alfred
motto: Now that Weigel has departed, Charlie is 'Oky's' right hand man, and aspires to be the State Entomologist in the far distant future. We hope he won't go bugs over it. He gives a foundation for both the Glee Club and the Orchestra, but on the Glee Club trip last winter, it was somewhat weakened by the presence of the gentler sex.

name: Benson, Maurice Haynes
motto: He that hath no beard is less than a man
description: When the Cedar of Lebanon is old enough to be turned out loose he will make an excellent farmer, if his work here is a criterion. Benson has traveled considerably in his short lifetime; once he even got as far as Springfield. His express purpose in going was to demonstrate the fact that North Country boys are not to be slighted when it comes to scientific farming.

name: Blatchford, George Nason
motto: Let the world wagge and take mine ease in myne Inne
description: Be it generally known that, although George is an Arts and Crafts man, he knows a thing or two about electrics. Collecting nickels for the "Hampton Beach Shore Line Electric Railway—b'gosh" developed a grasping tendency in Blatch, which crops out whenever he goes onto a baseball field. For winter sports George controls the fate of the Crescent and demonstrates the range of his ten-cent flute, said range being six notes and fourteen blocks. From humane motives "Jawge" sees to it that all bugle blowing at his sumptuous lodging house shall be under his personal supervision. Thus he is filling the niche ordained for him.

name: Brackett, Ralph Derochemont
motto: Thou hast no fantasies which busy nature draws in the minds of men
description: After graduating from Portsmouth High and spending a year at Exeter, Ralph managed to enter the two-year course. He specialized in arithmetic for a year and then entered with the four-year men. Among other things in his eventful career, Crap has been a king, joker, politician, gambler, and a fusser. He just cannot keep away from Exeter. Since his course for the "kitten-footed" in Doc Grant's army, he has taken an active part in the military department.

name: Bragdon, Doris Lillian
motto: How sweet are looks that ladies bend On whom their favours fall!
description: It always seemed strange to us that Doris didn't stay over for any of the N. H. C. entertainments but, as usual, we found that there was a reason—she's a commuter, too! Ask any of the girls what they think of her and they will invariably say, "Doris will do anything for you." In fact, she is one of the best-natured and most obliging girls in our class.

name: Brill, Milo Horace
motto: Well you—!
description: The Tanktown Canuck ostracised himself from Durham society his first two years. This, of course, was due to his promises and vows to remain true. His Junior year has seen a vast change. The gay white way has lured him to the folds of the 400. He has become a real society bud. "I wouldn't touch the vile weed." These were his words back when he was hardly weaned from high school days. Now the pesky weed is the only source of relief from the strain caused by walloping pots.

name: Broderick, Charles Bernard
description: Charlie is sometimes seen on the campus between trains. He is the kind that is built low to the ground, but you should see him tear up the sod when going around end. The social life at college has never appealed to Melba, but when he is in his own home town he changes to a regular butterfly. He is specializing in cheese-making (?) and we feel sure that he will make a success of the business.

name: Bugbee, Helen Marion
motto: A prettier maid 'twere hard to find
description: If this enthusiastic devotee of the Arts and Science course continues through life to be as good as she is pretty, she will do well. With a set of accounting books under one arm and a sociology book under the other, Betty Bug wends her way, casting smiles right and left. Although her smiles are sunny, unlike Aurora who loves the day, Betty seems fondest of the (K)night.

name: Butterfield, Byron Goodwin
motto: O! Landlord, fill the flowing bowl
description: This tall pine of the frizzle-top variety is famous for two utterly dissimilar qualities: climbing trees and sleeping through Chapel. The former habit may be inheritance from simian ancestors, but his lack of interest in Chapel speakers is said to be due to the fact that he once went to the Old Howard. That experience is still fresh in his memory—so fresh that mere lectures are "a beastly bore." "A bad state of affairs for a farmer," say we.

name: Cahalane, Reginald Foster
motto: Er-er-er, Down New York
description: Reg became president of the Freshman Class either by the grace of God or by the help of Bill Allen, we cannot tell which. At first he thought there was no school like the Dover kindergarten, but now his interest is centered on the Connecticut College for women. Cal came near being elected mayor of Durham Point, but the advent of the Ford caused him to lose all of his popularity. His greatest hobby is tending incubators and chasing dirt around the chem. building.

name: Came, Carleton Lord
motto: Lefty's gentle breeding and polished manners have won him a place with the Royalty. He doesn't always fit with 'King,' but the Queen—whenever she is—thinks Lefty is just about right. He reciprocates by going Berrying nearly every week, and we venture to predict that this diversion will continue to occupy his time and thoughts for a good long while. When you are ready Lefty, we'll be glad to act as ushers.

name: Cann, Frederick Bowman
motto: A man I am, crossed with adversity
description: The Chinaman came to us from East Jaffrey determined to specialize in agriculture, but later he thought he would prefer Descriptive, etc. Freddie wanted to become a real soldier, so he went to Plattsburg one summer, and since then he has been telling us how they did it out there. When needed, we expect to see the Chink in the Dramatic Club, impersonating Chin Lo Chun or some other Chinese god. A sleepy, dreamy, little man, until you start something and then, beware!

name: Clarke, Hugh Tressott
motto: Shall I, like a hermit dwell, On a rock or in a cell
description: Since his first day as a Freshman, Red has devoted all his time to the cause of sport for sport's sake, but his conception of the term has been such as to lead him to peculiar predicaments. Red has the distinction of being one of the first fellows to become dissatisfied with Fairchild Hall—and he moved. In athletics he is the only man to bowl a handicap match against himself, in undress uniform. Red also knows the rules of parliamentary procedure as handed down by a temporary student court.

name: Coburn, Charles Higgins
motto: My pride fell with my purse
description: Cobe became famous overnight as the result of the bitter trade-war known to the legal lights as College Shop vs. Coburn-Garland & Co. When the smoke cleared "Cobe" found that he had a better idea of commercialism and nine cents in change. He tried to change the manner of spelling his name to "CoLburn," the "L" being love for his opponent. Cobe is now thinking seriously of majoring in Economics, hoping thereby to become more familiar with margin of profit.

name: Coburn, Rodney Clyde
motto: Full long were his legges and full lene; Y'lyk a staff—there was no calf y' sene
description: It has been remarked that Rodney has gradually pined away since that memorable night when he went to Ballard Hall—only to meet with cold rebuke. His walks no longer lead him to green fields, but to Dover and Newmarket, where he strives to dance away his sorrow. Rodney has made good in his chosen work, but he is so reticent about it that he has moved his worldly goods from Cockroach Tavern to a hermit's abode down near the ocean, where he can be assured that his past history and present deeds will never become the property of the common herd.

name: Cummings, Charles
motto: Steady and strong he's plugging along
description: Chuck entered in the two-year course, but a year of it was enough and he changed over. Then he was seized with the desire to make up lost time and with the voracity of the shark that he is, he devoured course after course until he outstripped his adopted classmates and became a sure enough Senior. Now Chuck is getting old and serious, but he may be young yet. His faculty favorites have left, since he was especially fond of Bud and Pat, and he is still shedding tears over the low marks they gave him.

name: Cushing, Mary Olive
motto: She's pretty to walk with, And witty to talk with, And pleasant, too, to think on
description: Those socials back at the academy seem to hold attractions for Mary and she always says she enjoyed the "Trip" and hated to come back. We wonder why! Last year her failing was for red hair, but learning in Drawing 70 that red is exciting, by House Party time she preferred black hair. In spite of her fears of getting over-plump and losing her curls, Mary is invariably good-natured and ready for fun.

name: Dalton, Stanley Hatch
motto: What care I when I can lie and rest, Kill time and take life at its very best
clubs: Author - "When the Sands of Texas Grow Cold."
description: Samanthy celebrated his coming out in Durham Society by being seduced with a sack of Bull Durham and a Zig Zag. Shortly after, he decided that the Creator intended that he should be a son of the soil instead of an engineer. When "Woody" decided to take the Mexican Bull by the horns, Sammy showed his spirit by enlisting and spent his summer by bordering on despair. They say that he shot the bull, all right, but couldn't get away with it.

name: Dame, Ralph Leighton
motto: Patience is a virtue, that is what we claim; 'Tis seldom found in fellows, but always in a Dame
description: When Nig landed here from "Tanktown" he was as nice a "dame" as you would care to look at, but after living a whole year amid the smoke and odors of the Crescent, we had to dub him "Nig." He is a runner, of the rhizome type—hitting the turf for the quarter mile. "She may not be a Quaker, but at any rate she's a friend." How about it Nig? Ralph became famous his Freshman year by singing low down songs (not in quality but in pitch) at the "Hen-House."

name: Densmore, John Francis
description: John hasn't been the same lad since he left that neat and tidy home "out Lee Hill way." To become a bacteriologist with home laboratories was Densmore's ambition once, but since developing that enlightenment which comes to every Junior, he thinks that perhaps economics will be of more benefit to him— O hallowed be the momentous day, That John fell in with Brer' McKay.

name: Dodge, James William
motto: Thou art like some cross-grained, crabbed wight Who casts black looks on each delight
description: "Jim" brought two suit cases to Durham in his Freshman year. One contained a dress-suit and cornet; the other was bulging with a full-grown grouch. Despite his rustic training, "Jim" is able to hold his own at a social gathering and he plays the cornet "just beautifully."

name: Dreller, Louis
motto: A moderate Jew is a more confounding piece of anomaly than a wet Quaker
description: The Pride of Portsmouth and Sage of Durham is by rights a debater instead of an engineer. The way he says "Aw, Gee, Naw!" is a most convincing argument—to those who don't know him. Louis' favorite pastime used to be to wander into the far-famed Union, with only twelve minutes for dinner, and discuss everything from Home Ec. to Hydraulics, but since he has been rooming next door to Dummy, his jaw-wagging proclivities have ceased.

name: Durgin, Charles Butterworth
motto: I'll bet you five dollars
description: This lad is the one bird of the college after the women. "They all stoop to him with a box of fudge in one hand, and, when they have supplied a sufficient quantity, he confers the degree of "D.D." To date only one has been given. The white lights of Newmarket have taken a backseat in Chuck's diary, the Dover lights are much higher power. The attraction is indeed great at times, especially when it is time for a little relaxation.

name: Durgin, John Frank
motto: While diligently laboring in botany lab. one afternoon, it suddenly occurred to Durg that he would do a little research work in opposition to Billy Sunday, in order to bring the old town to the "wet" column.
description: Johnny has had high athletic aspirations. He is the main swing on the commuter hockey team, fullback on the football team of the same organization, and has his place cinched on the gym. team.

name: Eastman, Eva Emma
motto: Ain't I versatile
description: This might well apply to our little Eva. She is conscientious and fully intends to do her share at any time. She plays basketball and the mandolin, writes very witty sayings about her friends and acquaintances, and also plays tennis. However, the latter wasn't fully demonstrated until she went to Silver Bay last June. Then she played against the champion tennis player of Simmons and—but never mind the score.

name: Flynn, Mildred Margaretta
motto: Hail to thee, blithe spirit
description: Dover hasn't so many attractions but what Mildred is willing to forego them occasionally so as to favor us with her presence. One evening she visited us in the guise of a Japanese lady, but generally she comes clothed in her right mind. That "yes, dear," you hear as you pass the door of the Rest Room, belongs to her. Besides being good-natured, Mildred is intensely patriotic and, along with several others in that English 60 class, has an abiding faith in the powers of "Uncle Sam."

name: Fogg, Heman Charles
motto: If a 'Laddie' kiss a student With the sun a shining down, Would she kiss this same young student If a Fogg was hanging round?
description: Heemie first came into prominence his Freshman year, when he very modestly took a wrestling bout from the "Pride of Vermont" (Johnson) at Rand's Frat. Foggie is still a loyal member of the rough-neck bunch—"Rands." We wonder why. Famous for trailing the elusive jug of cider, and a young Ladd.

name: Ford, Joseph Basil Jr.
description: Right from the factory in Detroit, Basil came puffing, snuffing and grunting into Durham, but quickly lost a bearing one night when he ran into a pack of dogs, who took a very friendly attitude toward him. Since then he has been on the war-path for cats and has succeeded in capturing quite a number from the surrounding towns. Joe's favorite class is A. H. 55- "Henry, I want to get a good answer from you once in awhile." Answer?? The cause of his greatest worry is the rotten condition of the transportation facilities between here and Durham Point.

name: Foss, Edith Emily
motto: Plough deep while sluggards sleep
description: Edith is another for whom Durham has no attractions, except such as can be found in the library every afternoon. It is a queer but noticeable fact that whenever she works there, Fluffy studies at the same table. Home Economics is too mild for Edith and so she shines in such courses as Economics I, History, etc., along with other Arts and Crafts sharks.

name: Furber, Josephine Marguerite
motto: Few things are impossible to diligence and skill
description: Josephine studies hard, dances well and is an obliging friend—and what more can you ask for in a college girl? She is one of our several classmates from Laconia, City on the Lakes, and her favorite saying is, "If you think Laconia is nice in winter, you just ought to see it in summer." But she thinks Durham is pretty nice, too, and so we are glad she entered with us, glad she kept up with us, and glad that she will finish with us.

name: Garland, Otis Raymond
description: To look at "Red," one would never believe that he was given over to the frivolous things of life, but it is rumored that he has the summer-boarder business down pat. Here in Durham Red's behavior is of the best, as a rule, but once he slipped and fell. That was when his evil-eyed roommate induced him to encroach upon the hallowed domain of one local department store and curiosity shop. Since then Garland hasn't dared to venture into the eastern part of the town, except after dark, as he claims that a red-headed man is marked forever.

name: George, Maurice Channing
motto: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance
description: It's hard to name this being, But "Muc's" the name he's got; Yes, we named him "Mucous Membrane" And called him "Muc" for short. If you remember, "Membrane" Is a tissue-thin and weak, But take a look at "Mucous"- His very actions speak. Now "Muc" is counterbalanced By a brain that's almost dense, He hasn't grown to manhood; Why don't the boy commence?

name: Gilson, Marion Susan
motto: And when she smiles, another smiles And soon there's miles and miles of smiles
description: Jolly? Yes, and capable, whether in a midnight frolic or in her capacity as a nurse at the dormitory. Marion has a wide circle of friends both in the college and in surrounding towns. She came to New Hampshire College intent upon specializing in Home Economics, but after a year spent in teaching the young idea of how to shoot, she decided that to be a social worker was her vocation.

name: Gove, Lucile Adeline
motto: The pen is mightier than the sword
clubs: Chairman Dramatic Committee Liberal Arts Club (3)
description: In the two years that Lucile has been with us, we have discovered several things about her. To begin with, she has executive ability; then, too, she is no obscure light in dramatic circles; and finally as a writer, her fame has spread afar—even to Dean Academy, some inmate of which receives a majority of her contributions. We are sure The New Hampshire does not begrudge that, if it pleases her and doesn't take up too much of his time deciphering them.

name: Graham, Alfred Newman
motto: Pleasant the snaffle of courtship, improving the manners and carriage, But the colt who is wise will abstain from the terrible thornbit of marriage.—Kipling.
description: So sang a philosopher. Now, Al is a philosopher, but only time can tell whether he follows the advice of one of his kind. At any rate, he has faithfully watched the 6:12 train pull in nearly every night for a whole semester. His friends have always regretted that he elected to cast his lot with the Arts and Science grafters. Under Lefty Richards' guidance, however, he may fool us all yet and turn out to be a Bill Shakespeare or a Milton, and be famous in spite of himself—after he's dead.

name: Griswold, James Herbert
motto: Books! 'tis a dull and endless strife!
description: Jim was a bashful boy at first, but Rand's Frat soon made him into a first class rough-houser and window-smasher. Last year Jim made a short business trip to Boston and said nothing about it after he came back, but he was observed by a Durham detective who returned with unsavory reports. Ask Jim what they were. The Mexican Athletic Association has bid for Jim, but he is declared ineligible by the board of directors of Doc Grant's Army.

name: Hadley, Ruth Whitney
motto: Always witty, always wise
description: After wavering between home and the Dorm for two years, Ruth has decided to remain at Ballard, where she reigns as house president. The motto of Ballardites is, "If in doubt, consult Ruth;" but they are lucky if they find her, for she believes in outdoor exercise in daytime and sleep at night. It is still a mystery why she takes interest in the "Enty" department and what she considers her "moral support." She is conscientious, but is not lacking when there are good times going on.

name: Hall, Fred William
motto: Now for good-luck cast an old shoe after me
description: Fred's nom de plume was won through his extraordinary ability as a hardware salesman, with his field of action that well-known beat including Durham and Dover. With the outbreak of the war, Alumino abandoned his dishes and entered upon a more care-free existence as the pilot of a better known brand of hardware. Of course the language involved is of necessity not so pure, but it's educational "for a' that."

name: Hardy, Ernest Winfield
motto: He dwelt among the untrodden ways
description: "God's Country" has sent a man down here who will make his mark in the world provided he looks after his health. Being of the determined type, Hardy has forged to the front despite many serious drawbacks. Restrictions are not for him, so he induced his faithful roomie to forsake Pettee Block, which they loved so well, for quarters beyond the pale of the law. This serio-comic youth has demonstrated his ability as a rough-houser, student, debater of ponderous questions, actor, et cetera.

name: Harris, Florence Julia
motto: I am saddest when I sing
clubs: Delegate to Maine State Y. W. C. A. Conference (1)
Delegate to Silver Bay Y. W. C. A. Conference (2)
Annual Member of National Board of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: Enthusiasm plus rare humor and clever foresight is the keynote of our "long girl's" personality. We defy any spectator of the 1917-1918 girls' basketball game who had the courage to remain within range of her wildly flourished megaphone to contradict this statement. But whether it be organizing a girls' basketball team, rehearsing for the most doubtful of Dramatic Club plays, or collecting Y. W. C. A. dues, Flossie's energy is ever the same—persistent and unfailing.

name: Hewley, Hilbert Goodrich Jr.
motto: Up to Fabyans
description: Goody has been "round a bit" and, if you want information on anything, just ask him; then he will "lucidate." On the football field, or anywhere else in fact, he is conspicuous because of his rag-picker voice. Give him a piece of Wrigley's and watch him go!

name: Hoitt, Martha Luena
motto: She was ever fair and never proud, Had tongue at will, and yet was never loud
description: If ever a girl's name fitted her personality, Martha's name fits hers. Conscientious and dependable to the highest degree, we can only say of her, "May the best thing she wants be the worst thing she gets." Martha's greatest asset is that intent look of hers—each professor is so sure that his subject is her one great interest in life. On the contrary, her interests are varied and she finds time to enjoy fully the privileges of living at home in a college town.

name: Howe, George Merrill
motto: Jigger is the pride of 'Tookie,' and rightly so.
description: Take a squint at his map, is it not as solemn, sweet and pure and gentle as a ewe's? Jig is a romantic youth with a passion for two-wheeled motoring, provided he can take with him his Hardy valet. His motor car and sweet disposition have made him the idol of the co-eds and the despair of the entomology department. He has worked out original formulae for the extermination of the Oyster Shell Leak and the bothersome Molasses Bug and it is hoped that his life will be spared for graduate work.

name: Humiston, John Edwin
motto: He was wont to speak plain and to the purpose.
description: Ed came to college with many ideas in his head to be developed as time permitted. His ambitions rose from substitute janitor, wood-chopper, Dover Inspector, to sharking the Aggie Course, incidentally drowning Ben Butler in a jug of ink and applying for an N. H. as chapel assistant to the orchestra. Hummy's most valuable experience was his trip to the County Farm where visitors are fed on ensilage. But he can absorb text-books and baseball with the best of 'em, so why not allow him this innocent recreation, provided he can stomach it?

name: Huse, Irene Meyers
motto: Clear-headed, warm-hearted.
clubs: Manager and Treasurer Girls' Glee Club (3)
description: From the orderliness of her desk to her serene and polished poise at informals, Irene has shown herself to be a thoroughly efficient person. She can organize a successful walking club, and with equal facility arrange a trip for her friends to the school for feeble-minded in Laconia. We rather imagine that her next project is going to be a greenhouse for she carries on a lengthy correspondence with a truck gardener in Claremont.

name: Hutchinson, Perne Rawson
motto: I would fain die a dry death
description: It would not be sticking to the bald and naked truth to say, as that illustrious weekly, The Bingville Bugle, puts it: "P. R. is our reigning society king." He has been educated to mingle with the upper crust, but not finding it here, he consoles himself with indulgence in Town Talk, aided and abetted by Pa Curtis of "New Dorm" fame. When all's said and done Hutch is a pretty good fellow, ready to give a lift anytime, believing that "every knock is a boost."

name: Hutchinson, Raymond Walch
description: Reed's Ferry is one of the General Unknowns. Ray tried once to teach geography and personal history thusly: "Reed's is on that famous river Styx," and Ray was assistant pilot the summer of 1914. You must have gone downstream, Ray, poling capacity loads of evil ones with that good left wing. The patrons of the line couldn't help but be impressed with Hutch's good humor and willingness to oblige, as well as by his good commonsense and genuineness.

name: Hyde, Lincoln Spencer
description: Spence decided that the curriculum at Harvard was sadly incomplete, according to his views, and, consequently he came here in order to acquire some of Pa Taylor's pet schemes for becoming an agricultural king. Upon graduation he will settle down in his native town and show them how to rake away the stones and raise things.

name: Ingerson, Della May
motto:
A springy motion in her gait
A rising step, did indicate
Of pride and joy no common rate
description: If you want to know whether Della is in town or not go to "T" Hall. If she is there, you'll hear her merry laugh echoing down the corridors. There must be something nice about Exeter to affect one that way. Della often stays over for an informal or a football rally, but never if there is a dance going on "down the line." Life for her is "just one laugh after another."

name: Irvine, Howard Thompson
description: Skeet has floated in and out of town for the last three years. He is one of those quiet fellows who never say much but know a heap. On the football field he is the man that steps back to kick the ball sixty yards or more. Skeet is going to specialize in botany, although we feel sure that he would make a good horse doctor. However, we wish him luck in whatever field he enters.

name: Jeffers, Weston Harvey
motto: Now let me consider that for a while
clubs: Assistant News Editor The New Hampshire (2)
description: Who would have believed that the meek and unsophisticated Freshman who addressed upper classmen, and even profs, as "Sir," would two years later be imperiously demanding copy for the GRANITE, with threats of personal violence if the same were not immediately forthcoming? Yet so far has this Piker from Pike left the rabble and common herd behind, that if he should ever spare the time to die, he would unhesitatingly step up to St. Peter and from force of habit demand, "Got any copy for the GRANITE?" Jeff is the first man to wear squeaky slippers and still keep his haughty and dignified manner; but he is so kind-hearted that he would lose a night's sleep if he thought he had ever killed any time.

name: Jenkins, Rodney Solon
motto: Sink or swim, live or die, You'll find Jenks standing by
description: Jenks came to New Hampshire with the grim determination to make the football team and incidentally to learn a little about agriculture, and in both of these pursuits he has succeeded finely. In order to ring in on all of the social functions, Jenks has a special arrangement with Sam Runlett whereby he gets all of the invites addressed to anyone whose name begins with J. His most amusing side line is trimming Pony in agronomy prelims.

name: Jones, Russel Cyprian
motto: If frogs carried watchpockets and hens toted canes, I'd disguise my topnotch and fit with the 'Janes'
description: Stranger, if you ever see what appears to be a ball of fire shooting down the railroad track don't be alarmed. It is only "Jonsey" hanging onto the rear end of the local accommodation to Exeter.

name: King, Elsie Louise
motto: In ourselves our fortune lies; Life is what we make it
description: That such a wee slip of a girl should possess such an abundance of purposeful energy, searing hot temper, executive ability and general information (including the latest Dover gossip) surpasses our understanding. Elsie was the shining light of Chem. 51, until that fatal day when Stewart discovered her distributing doughnuts among her famished classmates. From that day forth her enthusiasm for chemistry waned perceptibly. "Some profs are so easily prejudiced!"

name: Lane, Merton Burgess
motto: Cushing Alma Mater, Alma Mater Cushing dear
description: Gib hails from Ashburnham, of the good old "Bay State." Jeeze was the original heart-breaker, but now he has settled down and is competing with Morrill for a place in the telephone office. He plugs the switchboard and chemistry equally well, but never the flues in the Captain's boilers. Shortly there will be a new thoroughfare in town called "Jeeze Lane" from the Theta Chi house to the telephone office. An efficient waiter, loyal friend, Mecca basketball player and a "good gent."

Shortly there will be a new thoroughfare in town called "Jeeze Lane" from the Theta Chi house to the telephone office. An efficient waiter, loyal friend, Mecca basketball player and a "good gent."

name: Lary, Erma Mosso
motto: Be thy own self always and thou art lovable
description: Even the darksome halls of old Ballard brightened with the coming of Erma, the dainty, the chic, the light-hearted; and within two weeks every Freshman girl there became a willing convert to the "Westbrook way" in maxixe, fudge and manner. But now, "Oima's" deft manipulation of the cut system is equaled only by her sleight-of-hand disappearance at the entrance of an outraged proctor on the scene of forbidden hilarity.

name: Libby, Donald Whitney
motto: Hospital cottages for feeble-minded children
description: His first years were spent in "hunting trouble" in the Newmarket exchange, spearing eels with the Palmers, attending Kewpie dances and taking Chem. The "King" and Perley got tired and sick of seeing his old gray trousers and army shoes and "passed him up" in Chem. 52. Now that his heart has been Pierced and 16-year-old girls are at a premium, he has resigned to the inevitable, cares for the sick and leaves the well alone, and gives Morrill and Lane the benefit of his experience from fussing the telephone operator.

name: Lynde, Leslie Eugene
motto: Some say he does, he says he doesn't, But we think he does, at that
description: Behold a composite sample of what Manchester and Dover High Schools can produce. In the winter Les is connected with Hose 3 of the Dover Fire Department, but in the summer he knocks down nickels on the Dover, Somersworth and Rochester Street Railway. He plays baseball and is mighty good on "Lynde" drives. He carries a slip stick, so we suppose he is an Engineer.

name: McConachie, Max
motto: A man without a country
description: Eats pork on Friday, therefore he can't be a Jew or an Irishman. Doesn't drink whiskey, therefore he isn't Scotch. He must be a "Ginny" for he likes to shine shoes in the College Shop. "Mick" says he can sing in any flat if he has the key, but you ought to hear him sing "The Campbells Are Coming" and B natural. You're all right, Max, but the next time you take ether, don't mention her name. Thumb-breaker; heart-breaker; author—Latest success, "Lost in Durham." Ask him.

name: Martin, Giles
motto: Cleanliness is next to Godliness
description: Back in '14, Giles fooled himself into thinking that he wanted to be a chemist—and he still believes it. This year Giles had a problem to solve. He needed money to pay for the damage he did in lab., and, since he had had some experience as a boy pushing wheelbarrows for the Manchester Highway Department, he chose an Oriental laundry as a monetary soothing syrup. In this clean game he spends time and earns money; in Chem he spends money and earns time—in jail.

name: Merrill, Lester Edwin
motto: If all would lead their lives in love like me, Then bloody swords and armor should not be
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (2)
description: Calm serenity and steadfastness characterize the Lover from Lebanon. A certain photograph he owns is enough to keep any man from wobbling into the slough of despond, and therefore he ought not to be expected to act like the average Junior, who thinks he is, but isn't, enamored of some fair damsel. Aside from this minor defect, Lester is a mighty good scout, ready to do his share in all worthwhile undertakings.

name: Merrill, Marguerite Mae
motto: The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength and skill
description: Marguerite's passion for promptness is almost equal to her talent for singing. Combined in her are the rare qualities of a Marathon runner and a Melba. Add to these the silver-tongued oratory of Cicero and you have Marguerite. In her person, Munchausen has found his peer. A vivid imagination, a ready wit and a knack for storytelling, furnish material for marvelous tales and original excuses. The profs are her unsuspecting victims and many an overcut and continuous tardiness are explained away by our silver-tongued co-ed.

name: Miltimore, John Everett
motto: Short but sweet
description: Although taking the Aggie course, Jack will probably become a night watchman, for he's a regular owl. Who would ever think to see him here that he was a ladies' man, but did you see him at the Boston College game? If you can't get a girl in the summertime you can't get a girl at all. John sings in the Glee Club, but in the church choir—. He lost one toe hurrying and has concluded that Better late than never is the best policy.

name: Morrill, Joseph Warren
motto: Come not within the measure of my wrath
description: Joe, alias Moose, drifted in from Andover and we have always been thankful that he came to New Hampshire. The fair sex never appealed to our athlete in former years, but he was magnetized to an informal this winter and now is considered a shining light in the social whirl. Joe majored in football and as captain he will have a heavy responsibility on his shoulders next fall. Here's success to you, Joe, in your line.

name: Morrill, Raymond Soule
description: Ray speaks several languages fluently—the latest profane! He tells us he would never live on a farm without a "Park." We don't know what hand he uses, but he is spare hand at the Durham telephone exchange. "Durham, are you waiting? Durham, are you through?"

name: Nevers, Ralph Poor
motto: "My son, if a maiden deny thee and scoffingly bid thee give o'er, Yet lip meets lip at the lastward—get out! She has been there before"—Kipling
description: Such was the advice given Ralph when the fire drove him out of Salem and into New Hampshire—and what good has it done him? With the ink not dry on the letter to the "girl he left behind," he'll stop on his way to the post office to talk with a Freshman co-ed and miss two mail trains. We'll have to admit, however, that Poor Ralph Nevers is rather smooth, even if he did try to be a chemist. A local minister told him once that his natural gifts branded him as a lawyer. His friends think that poor advice. As a chemist, he'll have to be honest with Nature, or get blown up some day.

name: Ordway, Frederick Ira Jr.
motto: Many a noble man has been nursed on Candia's bosom
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (1)
description: Freddie still maintains that Manchester is a suburb of Candia, and is the originator of the famous "elephant and bag of salt joke." He is a great golf enthusiast, usually playing with one stick. His specialty is "putting" with Gertie. Our Freddie is a bearcat with the women. "A model man" (model meaning a small imitation of the real thing).

name: Osborne, Carver Haines
motto: Still waters run deep
description: A specimen drifted up from the stern and rock-bound coast of Cape Cod and even as those before him, he was called a doctor. Ask him any Saturday afternoon where he is going, and invariably he will reply that he is going to visit his Aunt in Rochester. Doc isn't an athlete but he gets his exercise each year by taking Chem. 51. He is a good spender and lender, but says he will never lend an overcoat again.

name: Parsons, John Joseph
motto: His spiky locks time hath to silver turned
description: John is one of the best-natured fat men that ever got caught electing a course in Physics. He declares that Economics and Physics are not even distantly related, but he didn't find it out in season. Never mind, John, you are jolly when you're peeved.

name: Perley, Sidney Harold
motto: You fit with me, kid! Shake!
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (3)
description: Sid isn't a "Horti" student, but a "Gardner" in town has said that he is "some fusser." George almost won a prize with his oxen at Portsmouth Fair, but they got out of step and nearly got cast in front of the judges' stand. He didn't come from the Kimberly mines, but you ought to see his "Diamond." Angleface would make great strides in the society game, but is held back by his feet.

name: Richardson, Clesson Willard
motto: Men of few words are the best men
description: He doesn't smoke, he doesn't swear, He doesn't crib—he doesn't dare. He never looks at the co-eds gay, He never takes a holiday. What are his faults? I'll tell you all: He carries the mail to Morrill Hall!!

name: Sanders, Elmer Nason
motto: Generally speaking, what is a woman? Why, generally speaking—
description: So Sandy says and he knows. He also remarks that she is no parrot even if they do call her Polly. Pinkie is very careful of his eating for he has "Fletcherized" frequently of late. It may be hard for a "Read" to get along under adverse conditions, but if it has a good "sandy" backing I guess it will exist. Pole vaulter, old woman, good chemist, social lion (?) and all round good fellow. Here's wishing you all that's good, old top.

name: Sawyer, Lester Little
motto: How pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together in unity
description: Sawyer, appropriately named "Bull," has had a wild and rugged career. His Freshman year he terrorized the cook at the Sawyer House; his Sophomore year he had his hair cut pompadour and it lasted, without combing, till he went to Plattsburg. "Bull" thinks nothing of dragging a pig downstairs by the hind legs or wringing a chicken's neck. He has broken more dishes, "K'd" more exams, slept through more Chapels and drunk more cider than any other man in college. It is mean to kid a man of his past, but we must add his greatest performance: that of being the first "18" man to visit Ballard Hall. Strange to say, he hasn't been there since, though "the memory lingers."

name: Sawyer, Robert Hamilton
description: Bud came to us right from the Friends' School of Baltimore. He has always been prominent because of his wisdom; however, being "wise" never got him by the Sophomores' paddles. His business aspirations made him the agent of Chu Hing Gum, and Robert with his little laundry toboggan is a sight which warms many a co-ed's heart.

name: Scott, Susan Helen
high school: Robinson Seminary, Skidmore School of Arts
motto: I am restless and athirst for far-away things
description: Sue's one venture away from New Hampshire College has made her long for new worlds to conquer and she feels that her education will be incomplete without a course at one of our western universities. She occasionally attends our college activities, but after hearing her discourse on "Why Men Bore Me," we realize why her presence is only occasional. Never mind, Sue, we hope you will stay with us another year.

name: Scovell, Earl Louis
description: It would be a pertinent question, indeed, to ask Scovell why he commutes clear from Haverhill. Possibly some female society wants to keep that crinkly, fluffy, golden-yellow hair within sight. Scovell belongs in the group of Unknowns, but apparently he doesn't worry about it. When he gets rich he is going to buy up the B. & M. between Durham and Haverhill and throw it into the Atlantic, so great is his appreciation of that concern's service.

name: Sleeper, Clarence Wilson
motto: By jingoes, you gotter hand it to me, kid
description: When Clancy ordered "Phaseoli" in a respectable Italian restaurant he was told to descend one flight to the tap room. (He only emerges, "De profundis," to accompany Brother Hawes with an empty cocktail bottle on deputation trips.) He's a "Boomer" in all he undertakes, but he likes to get his "Shuttleworth." Vocalist of kitchen range. No use. You can't play, if you don't hold 'em. Slough 'im Clancy!

name: Spencer, Archie Mcquesten
motto: In peace there's nothing so becomes a man as modest stillness and humility
description: Usually a conservative youth, attempting only the practical and possible, Archie once only exceeded the bounds of reason by attempting to jump Pettee Creek at its widest point—about eleven feet. He didn't have his slip-stick handy and couldn't work a transit, so he brought up in the cooling waters of Durham's lovely stream. Now Archie is a total abstainer from liquid refreshments. To guard against further recklessness he has placed himself under the care of Town Father Hoitt.

name: Stearns, Sam Loring
motto: Honesty is the best policy
description: From the Queen City he came wearing a smile that will never come off and bringing a full measure of good-naturedness that has won him unbounded friendship. As a Freshman he possessed an inordinate desire to learn every word in Webster's Dictionary; as a Sophomore he commenced dabbling in society, but won his laurels when he boxed Stimson out back of the "Tin Palace" one evening; and as a Junior he suddenly cast his lot with "Hen" Mitchell and is now ardently learning the poultry business. Sam's only faults are falling up stairs and occasionally raving about some pretty Dover queen.

name: Stimson, Robert Chapman
motto: Nature has framed strange fellows in her day
description: When this big hunk of humanity blew into town it was whispered among the populace "Who is this man of might?" After nearly three years we reply, "The eater of eats." Stim loves only two things in this wide world, one is feeds and the other is feeding. On one occasion he did conceive a temporary affection for a piazza post, but he soon returned to his first love. When a man has chemistry, electricity and social service at his ready command, he should have no trouble plowing his own paths, no matter how large the feet.

name: Swain, Lewis Churchill
motto:
The man whose silent days
In harmless joys are spent,
Whom hopes cannot delude
Nor sorrow discontent
description: A long, careful look at this chap acts as a soothing syrup for shattered nerves. One thinks of the deep, holy quiet of virgin forests, of calm lakes, of glorious but solemn sunsets when Swain's countenance occupies the field of vision. May the same calmness, modesty and silent industry be his "till man works no more."

name: Wadleigh, Clarence Benjamin
motto: And the muscles of his mighty arms were strong as iron bands
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (3)
description: This flaxen-haired athlete came to us from "down Exeter way" and, evading the notoriety attached to that bunch, has lived and worked quietly and effectively with us. But at rare intervals he delves deep into the recesses of his past and babbles incoherently of girls and strange love affairs, and even of a summer he spent "bell-hopping," just for the experience you know.

name: Walker, Ethel May
motto: A brave heart and a sunny smile
description: "Keep smiling; keep your temper; study, don't dream." These are Ethel's marching orders and she certainly lives up to them. Last year she developed a great interest in basketball and made her debut in the Freshman-Sophomore game, where she ran about making brilliant plays, smiling serenely all the while. By the way, it is whispered that Ethel would rather ride in the college "Black Maria" than in a Pierce-Arrow.

name: Waterman, Clarence Stanley
description: The Big Swede came to N. H. C. with quite a "rep" from Maine, and immediately became famous for his "loving" disposition, curly hair and football ability. Widder's coaxing little smile brought forth this comment from the co-eds: "I think he is awfully handsome and a swell football player, but—." The Peroxide Blonde attends church regularly, dotes on Bible quotations and has a reputation for law and order which presages a glorious career for him.

Nothing worse than playing cards with the Exeter bunch was ever known of him. There is a motto in his room which reads, "Be good and you'll be happy."

motto: When I was at home, I was in a better place, but travellers must be content

Still, he makes a weekly trip homeward, crooning, "Mother is the best sweetheart after all, There never was a girl so true!"

motto: With malice toward none and charity for all.
description: Last year, Hazel was a firm believer in the theory that fresh air and walking are good for the health, and so she and Mildred could be seen almost any noon hour strolling down the main street in the direction of the Gamma house. This year, however, she seems to have gone back on that theory and prefers to spend her leisure time playing whist in T Hall. text:
With malice toward none and charity for all.
Last year, Hazel was a firm believer in the theory that fresh air and walking are good for the health, and so she and Mildred could be seen almost any noon hour strolling down the main street in the direction of the Gamma house. This year, however, she seems to have gone back on that theory and prefers to spend her leisure time playing whist in T Hall. Unfortunate enough to live in Dover, Hazel has had to join the ranks of errand boys for the dorm girls, but good nature is one of her strong points and she apparently doesn't mind.

name: Howard, Willand
motto: Tobacco is a filthy weed— I love it
description: Sunshine entered as a two-year, then changed to a special, and he's a Junior now. Hence his motto- "Variety is the spice of life." His favorite song was, "Good-night, nurse," until "Along came Ruth." Why is it so dark on winter mornings? Sunshine rises late—no 8 o'clock. Laugh and the world laughs with you, with the window open you can hear it a mile.

name: Young, Claiborne Hart
motto: Thy body's short, thy neck is small, Thy head is greater far than all
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (3)
description: Clabby's time is divided—like Gaul—into three parts, to wit: studying, controlling the destiny of the College Greenhouse, and taking lessons in Philosophical Discourse from his room-mate. Whenever he has a spare moment he devotes it to the Terpsichorean art and it is even rumored that he takes a correspondence course in it summer vacations. Although Clabby is no giant physically he possesses enough good-naturedness for a man three times his size. We have yet to find him in a disagreeable mood.

name: Howe, Fred John
description: Fred is one of those fellows naturally gifted with the faculty of not worrying. He slides along nonchalantly through the semesters; at the end of each one he takes a little mental hop and catches up with the tail-enders. Fred was never known to get angry, he vents his wrath via his caustic tongue, but in spite of this he will make a first-class office boy for his hometown photographer.
